🤖 AI ANALYSIS — 🥈 Silver
11 Jun 2026 • 08:41 CET



# Silver Institutional Analysis

Silver is trading at 64.59, showing minimal daily change but positioned within a critical corrective structure. The Elliott Wave framework identifies Wave A as the first corrective leg at Primary degree, suggesting this decline is part of a larger downward correction within an overarching bullish trend, though current bias remains weakly bullish at only 30%.

The technical picture presents severe bearish momentum. RSI at 12.9 indicates extreme oversold conditions, historically associated with capitulation selling or exhaustion. MACD remains dee

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
ply negative at -1.29, confirming sustained bearish momentum without immediate reversal signals. The absence of bullish or bearish divergence removes potential early-warning indicators for trend changes.

Price structure relative to moving averages shows significant weakness. Trading approximately 12% below the SMA-20 (73.68) and 15% below the SMA-50 (75.74), silver demonstrates strong bearish pressure. However, proximity to the SMA-200 (67.05) suggests limited downside before encountering longer-term support.

The suggested trade targets 62.73 with a stop at 71.56, yielding an unfavorable 0.3:1 risk-reward ratio. This poor rat

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
io typically discourages institutional participation unless position sizing accommodates higher risk tolerance. The target aligns with further Wave A extension, while Fibonacci levels at 84.09 (61.8%) and 98.30 (38.2%) remain distant upside objectives.

Probability assessment favors continued corrective decline (60-65%) given momentum exhaustion and Wave A structure completion requirements. However, extreme oversold RSI creates reversal risk (35-40%) should buying interest emerge at current levels. ATR of 2.13 suggests moderate volatility, requiring careful position management through this corrective phase.
